title: Paramapania lucbanensis

The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. It is a member of the genus Paramapania, which is a group of orchids that are primarily found in Southeast Asia. The plant is classified as a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants for support, and it is commonly found in tropical regions. Its classification also includes the family-level classification as Orchidaceae and the order-level classification as Asparagales.
